free_text: 15 mm thick for detecting particles with energies
above 15 MeV. Two NaI(TI) crystals 0.14 mm and 2.62
mm thick were placed in the vacuum chamber together
with the irradiated targets for detecting particles
The paper describes photoreactions induced by
bremsstrahlung with different values of Eg-max
from 30 MeV to 90 MeV and resulting in the emission
of deuterons in a broad energy interval. The relative
photodeuteron yield was measured as function of the
nuclear mass number and the particle energy for a
fixed value of Eg-max and also as function of
Eg-max. The angular distributions of the
photodeuterons and the excitation function for the
given to some possible mechanism of the reaction
with photodeuteron emission. It is shown that the
cross section for the (gamma,d) reaction has an
exceeding the energy threshold of the reaction with
the emission of a single deuteron by approximately
the nucleon binding energy in the residual nucleus.
